phenotypes.label: ER-positive breast cancer
phenotypes.description: Female breast malignancy dominates, frequently with estrogen receptor positive status. Benign breast conditions (fibrocystic change, ductal hyperplasia) and lymphedema co-occur; a subset shows interstitial lung disease or immune thrombocytopenia.

phenotypes.label: Diabetes with ocular complications
phenotypes.description: Predominantly type 2 diabetes with prominent ocular involvement—nonproliferative and proliferative diabetic retinopathy with macular edema, glaucoma, retinal detachment, and vision loss. Peripheral neuropathy and microalbuminuria co-occur with episodes of hypo- and hyperglycemia, consistent with advanced microvascular complications.

phenotypes.label: Neurogenic bladder and recurrent urinary infections
phenotypes.description: Cluster characterized by neurogenic bladder and bowel, often in the setting of spina bifida and spinal deformity, leading to recurrent urinary tract infections, bacteriuria, and E. coli isolation. Associated features include dysuria and flank or coccygeal pain. This reflects neurogenic lower urinary tract dysfunction with infectious complications.

phenotypes.label: Osteoporosis and fragility fractures
phenotypes.description: Cluster centers on osteoporosis/osteopenia with distal radius, carpal, and pathological fractures. Secondary contributors include hyperparathyroidism from parathyroid adenoma with calcium/phosphate disturbances and chronic kidney disease–related mineral bone disorder. Hematologic conditions (multiple myeloma, B‑cell lymphoma, myelodysplasia) and post‑transplant immunodeficiency also appear, consistent with secondary osteoporosis and increased fracture risk.

phenotypes.label: Psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis
phenotypes.description: Psoriatic disease predominates, with cutaneous psoriasis (including scalp involvement) and psoriatic arthritis/arthropathy. Co-occurring inflammatory skin and ocular surface conditions such as rosacea, blepharitis, and dry eye are common, reflecting extra-cutaneous involvement.

phenotypes.label: Urologic disorders and urinary malignancies
phenotypes.description: Urologic conditions cluster featuring benign prostatic hyperplasia with lower urinary tract symptoms, erectile dysfunction, hematuria, urethral stricture, and raised PSA. Concurrent urinary tract malignancies are prominent, including bladder and kidney cancers, with related findings such as flank pain, renal lesions, varicocele, inguinal hernia, and pelvic organ prolapse.

phenotypes.label: HIV with STI and anal HPV
phenotypes.description: HIV infection with frequent STI co-infections (syphilis, gonorrhea, genital herpes) and HPV-related anal disease such as condyloma, anal dysplasia, proctitis, and perianal pain. Represents HIV-positive patients with concurrent sexually transmitted infections and anal HPV complications.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: This set mixes peripheral arterial and aneurysmal disease (peripheral vascular disease with intermittent claudication, atherosclerosis of the extremities, abdominal/thoracic aortic aneurysm or dissection) with laryngeal disorders (vocal cord paralysis, dysphonia). It also includes sexual activity status, substance use, and autism spectrum disorder, without a single unifying clinical mechanism.

phenotypes.label: IBD with hidradenitis and atopy
phenotypes.description: Ulcerative colitis and colonic Crohn’s disease predominate, with Clostridioides difficile colitis appearing as a gastrointestinal complication. Co‑occurring hidradenitis suppurativa, atopic dermatitis, allergic conjunctivitis, and sinonasal disease indicate an atopic/immune‑mediated pattern; Achilles tendinopathy/bursitis suggests IBD‑associated spondyloarthropathy with enthesitis.

phenotypes.label: Breast symptoms with galactorrhea and prolactinoma
phenotypes.description: Breast-related conditions dominate, including breast pain, nipple retraction, axillary mass, and nonspecific breast findings. Galactorrhea occurring alongside pituitary adenoma points to prolactin-driven endocrine causes for some presentations, consistent with breast disorders ranging from benign disease to malignancy.

phenotypes.label: Menopause with migraine and autoimmune comorbidity
phenotypes.description: Menopausal status with decreased libido and sexual desire codes co-occur with migraine (including aura and vestibular variants) and vasomotor rhinitis. Autoimmune/inflammatory comorbidities such as celiac disease and psoriasis are present, along with arthralgia/enthesopathy and elevated CRP, suggesting an inflammatory diathesis in midlife women.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine mixed sleep apnea, cerebral artery occlusion, and paroxysmal tachycardia with unrelated findings such as facial cellulitis and syphilis testing. This spans multiple systems without a single clinical pathway, so it does not form a coherent clinical pattern for selection.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The top conditions blend ubiquitous chronic comorbidities and nonspecific symptoms (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, diabetes, pain, nausea) with scattered unrelated findings (ocular and wrist injuries, ARDS, aspiration pneumonitis, paralytic ileus). There is no shared clinical theme, so this does not represent a coherent condition cluster.

phenotypes.label: Chronic comorbidity catch-all
phenotypes.description: Hypertension, hyperlipidemia, type 2 diabetes, GERD, chronic pain, anxiety/depression, obstructive sleep apnea, hypothyroidism, osteoarthritis, and obesity co-occur, with coronary atherosclerosis also present. This represents the broad multimorbidity baseline in adults with dementia during early follow-up, not a distinct organ‑system syndrome.

phenotypes.label: Spine pain with somatic dysfunction
phenotypes.description: Mechanical spine pain with radiculopathy and myofascial involvement. The pattern includes chronic low back and neck pain, sciatica, muscle spasm, and regional somatic dysfunction across cervical, thoracic, lumbar, sacral, and pelvic areas. This reflects degenerative or postural spine disease with radicular symptoms and associated cervicogenic headache or migraine.

phenotypes.label: Epilepsy with intracranial pathology
phenotypes.description: Neurologic pattern centered on seizures and epilepsy (generalized and focal, including refractory forms) with prominent focal deficits such as aphasia, dysarthria, hemiparesis, and ataxia. Intracranial pathology is common—hemorrhage, hydrocephalus, cerebral edema/compression, and ischemic stroke—consistent with structural brain disease precipitating seizures.

phenotypes.label: Lewy body dementia with RBD
phenotypes.description: Pattern consistent with dementia with Lewy bodies accompanied by REM sleep behavior disorder, cognitive impairment, and parkinsonian motor features (incoordination, abnormal posture, balance impairment). Pelvic floor dysfunction suggests autonomic involvement typical of synucleinopathies, with restless legs and disordered iron metabolism as associated sleep–movement features.

phenotypes.label: Prosthetic or transplanted heart valve
phenotypes.description: Presence of a prosthetic or transplanted heart valve with accompanying aortic and general valvular abnormalities. This reflects structural valvular heart disease with documented valve replacement/transplant status during the observed period.

phenotypes.label: Acute symptom catch-all
phenotypes.description: Nonspecific, cross-system acute complaints dominate, including chest pain, dyspnea/wheezing, abdominal pain with nausea, vomiting and diarrhea, generalized pain, and bleeding. This reflects broad, encounter-driven symptom coding rather than a single underlying condition, serving as the common acute presentation baseline.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The listed conditions do not reflect a unified clinical syndrome. A urinary tract infection term is mixed with generic chronic diagnoses and scattered injury or ocular findings, without a consistent clinical pathway.

phenotypes.label: Abdominal bloating and flatulence
phenotypes.description: Pattern of gastrointestinal gas complaints: abdominal bloating/distension, flatulence/eructation, gas pain, and passing flatus, with occasional nonspecific esophageal symptoms. Gynecologic pain codes (dysmenorrhea, irregular menses) co-occur in a subset, suggesting cyclic abdominal discomfort in some women.

phenotypes.label: Peripheral neuropathy and neuralgia
phenotypes.description: Peripheral nerve disorders with prominent neuralgia and inflammatory neuropathy are central, with hand involvement and other focal pain complaints. Low vitamin B12 appears in some, supporting deficiency-related neuropathy as a contributor. Overall this reflects peripheral neuropathic pain syndromes.

phenotypes.label: Obstetric and gynecologic conditions
phenotypes.description: Pregnancy-related conditions and complications dominate, including trimesters, high-risk pregnancy, preeclampsia, premature labor, specific gestational ages, and postpartum hemorrhage. Also present are gynecologic disorders such as ovarian cysts, polycystic ovary syndrome, vaginitis/bacterial vaginosis, abnormal Pap smear, abnormal uterine bleeding, uterine fibroids, pelvic pain, miscarriage, and vaginal discharge.

phenotypes.label: Lower-leg fractures with speech impairment
phenotypes.description: Prominent lower-extremity long-bone fractures (tibia/fibula) with late effects, chronic pain, stiffness, delayed union, and contracture cluster with communication deficits (difficulty talking, voice production problems) and neurologic sequelae. This pattern suggests polytrauma or intracranial injury with resulting speech impairment alongside orthopedic sequelae and reduced mobility.

phenotypes.label: Mixed falls and dermatology
phenotypes.description: Two unrelated patterns are combined. One cluster reflects falls and frailty—impaired mobility and balance, syncope/near-syncope, limb lacerations/abrasions, and vertebral compression fracture. The other consists of dermatologic diagnoses such as acne and melasma, which are not clinically linked to the falls pattern, so there is no single unifying theme.

phenotypes.label: Lumbar disc disease and back injury
phenotypes.description: Degenerative lumbar disc disease with associated acute and chronic back injuries predominate, with occasional retrolisthesis and cervical spondylosis. The pattern reflects spine-related mechanical pathology; abdominal wall hernia and uterine calcification appear as concurrent comorbid conditions.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions reflect generic symptoms and common chronic comorbidities without a unifying clinical story. The contrasted high‑lift items are scattered and unrelated (injuries, ocular findings, ARDS, neurologic scale entries), so this cluster is not clinically interpretable.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: Leading conditions combine valvular heart disease with oncologic and endocrine findings (lymphoma, abnormal mammogram, thyroid nodule) plus constitutional symptoms. These span unrelated clinical domains rather than a single coherent phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Cataract, glaucoma, vitreoretinal disorders
phenotypes.description: Age-related ophthalmic disease cluster featuring nuclear cataracts, posterior vitreous detachment/degeneration with retinal tears or detachment, and ocular hypertension/glaucoma, with macular degeneration and myopia also represented. This pattern reflects patients with substantial vision changes and risk to the retina and optic nerve, warranting close ophthalmic follow-up.

phenotypes.label: Foot drop with mixed comorbidities
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions point to a neurologic/spine process with foot drop, limb weakness, lumbosacral radiculopathy, and post‑laminectomy syndrome, sometimes alongside brain lesions. At the same time, there are unrelated findings such as high‑risk HPV/ASC‑US cervical abnormalities, lactose malabsorption, substance use, and ocular complaints, which do not form a single clinical story. This heterogeneity limits clinical interpretability as a unified phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Retinal and macular disorders with cataract
phenotypes.description: Ophthalmic cluster centered on retinal and macular pathology: epiretinal membrane, macular hole, cystoid macular change, lattice degeneration, subretinal fluid, and early age-related macular degeneration with central scotoma. Co-occurring secondary/incipient cataract, iridocyclitis, and steroid-related glaucoma suspicion are present, with visual field and acuity deficits such as transient vision loss and hemianopia.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine very common chronic comorbidities with scattered, unrelated injuries and acute findings. There is no shared clinical theme, so this cluster is not clinically interpretable as a single condition pattern.

phenotypes.label: Cutaneous neoplasms and pigmented lesions
phenotypes.description: Cluster of dermatologic conditions dominated by actinic keratoses, multiple melanocytic nevi (including dysplastic), seborrheic keratoses, hemangiomas, and skin cancers such as squamous cell carcinoma and melanoma. Occasional thyroid cancer and hypothyroidism codes appear, but the pattern centers on cutaneous neoplasia and pigmented lesions.

phenotypes.label: Bipolar spectrum with suicidality and injury
phenotypes.description: Bipolar spectrum and related mood/anxiety disorders with prominent manic or hypomanic episodes. The pattern includes suicidality, overdose, and traumatic injuries (including burns), alongside insomnia and psychotic features. Overall, it reflects psychiatric instability with elevated self-harm and safety risk.

phenotypes.label: Post-concussion syndrome and TBI sequelae
phenotypes.description: Traumatic brain injury and concussion with postconcussion syndrome predominate, accompanied by post‑traumatic headache, dizziness, and head injury sequelae. Prominent neuropsychiatric and cognitive symptoms are recorded, including inattention/ADHD, anxiety, PTSD, and mood/personality disorders, with vestibulo‑ocular findings such as convergence insufficiency and exophoria. This reflects patients with head trauma and persistent cognitive, behavioral, and visual sequelae.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: Nonspecific acute symptoms (chest pain, shortness of breath, nausea) appear alongside scattered injuries, exam findings, and unrelated conditions. There is no unifying clinical theme to support a distinct condition pattern.

phenotypes.label: Psychosis with suicidal/homicidal ideation
phenotypes.description: Severe psychiatric illness marked by hallucinations, delusions, and schizophrenia/schizoaffective spectrum diagnoses, often alongside major depressive episodes with psychotic features. Suicidal and homicidal ideation are prominent, with frequent anxiety/PTSD, insomnia, psychomotor agitation, and extrapyramidal signs; substance dependence is common. This reflects high‑risk psychosis with comorbid mood disorders.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: Top conditions mix common chronic comorbidities (hypertension, diabetes, hyperlipidemia) with nonspecific symptoms (chest pain, nausea, shortness of breath) and scattered unrelated findings. There is no coherent clinical theme to guide selection, so this is not a usable clinical cluster.

phenotypes.label: Systemic sclerosis with overlap myositis
phenotypes.description: Autoimmune connective tissue disease centered on systemic sclerosis and localized scleroderma, often with overlap myositis. Associated features include gastrointestinal dysmotility with diarrhea, cardiac conduction block, calcium metabolism disturbances, and non-scarring alopecia.

phenotypes.label: Outlet obstruction, nail infections, thrombophilia
phenotypes.description: This combines lower urinary tract obstruction from benign prostatic hyperplasia (urinary retention, bladder neck obstruction) with skin and nail infections or finger injuries (paronychia, cellulitis, ingrown nails). It also includes inherited thrombophilias (Factor V Leiden, prothrombin G20210A) and hemochromatosis, reflecting unrelated processes rather than a single clinical phenotype.
